feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Martha Lillard, last US polio patient using iron lung, dies at 78 in Oklahoma
entry.summary: Martha Lillard, the last U.S. polio patient using an iron lung, has died at 78 in Oklahoma

entry.title: What are ‘Ozempic feet’? Doctors warn of surprising weight-loss side effect
entry.summary: Doctors have recently seen a trend where significant weight loss can leave the feet looking bonier, looser, or more deflated.

entry.title: Another US citizen tests positive for Ebola virus while working in Congo amid record outbreak
entry.summary: A second U.S. citizen tested positive for Ebola while working for a humanitarian organization in the Democratic Republic of the Congo amid the outbreak.

entry.title: Outbreak of diarrhea caused by cyclosporiasis spreading in US with cases reported in CA, CDC says
entry.summary: Michigan has the highest number of cases, followed by Ohio and New York.

entry.title: Health officials warn of possible measles exposure after second confirmed case
entry.summary: Nashville health officials are warning residents who visited seven businesses between July 1 and July 4 that they may have been exposed to measles.

entry.title: Simple sitting change linked to lower risk of cancer death, study finds
entry.summary: Each additional hour of prolonged sedentary behavior was linked to a 10% higher cancer death risk, but brief movement breaks may significantly reduce it.

entry.title: There Are 2 Biologicially Distinct Forms of Major Depression, Says New Study
entry.summary: Major depressive disorder (MDD) is a severe, clinical type of depression, a more broadly defined mood disorder associated with a persistent feeling of sadness and a loss of interest.

entry.title: Guggenheim Museum among NYC buildings that tested positive for Legionnaires’ amid disease outbreak
entry.summary: The Guggenheim Museum was among a number of Manhattan buildings that recently tested positive for the bacteria that causes Legionnaires’ disease amid the city’s latest outbreak. The city health department on Friday released a list of 31 buildings ordered to c…

entry.title: Non-Invasive Brain Stimulation 'Significantly' Improves Depression in Just 10 Days, Trial Finds
entry.summary: Major depressive disorder is the most common mental health issue in the world, affecting hundreds of millions of people at some point in their lives.

entry.title: People Share The "Healthy Habits" That Actually Ruined Their Lives
entry.summary: "I injured my neck and gave myself a horrible, unstable tinnitus that ruined my life."

entry.title: 15 People Tried Popular Healthy Habits, And They Backfired In The Worst Ways
entry.summary: "I injured my neck and gave myself a horrible, unstable tinnitus that ruined my life."

entry.title: Last U.S. polio patient with an iron lung dies at 78 in Oklahoma
entry.summary: Martha Lillard had just turned 5 when she was diagnosed with polio and depended on an iron lung to live.

entry.title: Brain implant that stops seizures is life-changing for some epilepsy patients
entry.summary: One hundred epilepsy patients at Intermountain Health have been treated with a brain implant designed to stop seizures as they are starting.

entry.title: Male fertility crisis? Israeli global study finds 54% drop in testosterone levels over five decades
entry.summary: A large meta-analysis, studying five decades, has found a dramatic and sustained decline in testosterone levels among men, raising concerns that the male fertility crisis is deeply misunderstood.

entry.title: Cyclospora, the ‘Explosive Diarrhea’ Parasite, Cases Reported in at Least 31 States: See the Map
entry.summary: Explosive diarrhea parasite cyclosporiasis is causing possible outbreaks in half of US states. See a map of the rising rates from NBC News and the CDC.

entry.title: An anti-inflammatory diet may protect against dementia in some older adults
entry.summary: An anti-inflammatory diet rich in leafy greens and sardines may offer even more protection against dementia for some older adults than the highly regarded Mediterranean diet.

entry.title: Guggenheim among 31 Upper East Side sites that tested positive for Legionella bacteria
entry.summary: Owners of 19 of the buildings have already completed the process of cleaning and disinfecting their contaminated water-cooling towers.

entry.title: Dead crow cues 'wake-up call' for West Nile virus in California
entry.summary: A dead crow that tested positive for West Nile virus in Menlo Park recently prompted alarm amid a spike in infected birds across California.

entry.title: The ticks you may find in your state, and the diseases they can carry
entry.summary: It's been a gnarly year for ticks already in the U.S., but the ticks you're seeing will vary depending on where you live.

entry.title: Experimental drug reverses severe fatty liver disease by repairing the gut
entry.summary: An experimental drug called DT-109 reversed severe fatty liver disease in animal studies by repairing the gut and preventing harmful toxins from damaging the liver. The discovery could open the door to a new class of treatments for MASH and potentially other …

entry.title: UWS Building’s Hot Water System Tests Positive for Legionella Bacteria, Memo Shows
entry.summary: Legionella under a microscope. Wikimedia Commons/CDC. By Gus Saltonstall An Upper West Side building's hot water system has tested positive

entry.title: New Study Says Doing This for 2 Hours a Week Will Help You Live Longer
entry.summary: Getting a certain amount of resistance training a week may extend your lifespan

entry.title: From booksmaxxing to looksmaxxing: Why the viral trends concern some mental health experts
entry.summary: People are self-optimizing everything — protein, skincare, fitness — to such a degree that "maxxing" is one of social media's most popular suffixes.

entry.title: Is it normal to wake up at night to pee? Here’s what experts say.
entry.summary: Simple lifestyle changes can often help if nighttime waking is affecting your quality of life.

entry.title: A mom's headaches and nausea were dismissed as pregnancy symptoms. She had a brain tumor.
entry.summary: Jenney Bitner feared she wouldn't get to see her children grow up after a tumor in her brain revealed she had Stage IV melanoma.

entry.title: A neurologist shares 1 exercise you should try to outsmart brain aging
entry.summary: The best longevity secret may be a power walk. A neurologist explains how moving fast helps protect the brain and body from aging.

entry.title: 30-Day High-Protein Diabetes-Friendly Meal Plan for Weight Loss, Created by a Dietitian
entry.summary: This 30-day plan features a month of high-protein, diabetes-friendly meals and snacks at a lower calorie level to promote weight loss.

entry.title: Only 1 Connecticut town has detected West Nile virus every year for the past 5 years
entry.summary: Experts say West Nile virus is repeatedly detected along Connecticut's coastline and in the populated Hartford area.

entry.title: Precautions to take as cyclosporiasis cases in Michigan exceed 1,500
entry.summary: As cyclosporiasis cases continue to rise rapidly in Michigan, state health officials and the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention have provided precautionary tips while the investigation into the direct cause of the parasitic illness is ongoing.

entry.title: Georgia Woman Becomes First in the State to Receive New Treatment After a Heart Failure Diagnosis at 37
entry.summary: The rare surgery saved her life after being told she had just days to live

entry.title: Breakthrough Israeli study finds potential path to reversing hearing loss
entry.summary: The researchers identified a unique biological mechanism that could enable the regeneration of sensory hair cells in the inner ear – a process previously thought to be impossible in humans.

entry.title: US citizen working in DR Congo tests positive for Ebola
entry.summary: A US citizen working for a humanitarian group in the Democratic Republic of Congo has tested positive for the Bundibugyo strain of the Ebola virus, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said in a statement on Friday.

entry.title: 'Endometriosis tests would have given me years back'
entry.summary: It has been announced that two new rapid tests for the condition will be made available on the NHS.

entry.title: Common Hormonal Contraceptive Linked to Risk of Rare Brain Tumor
entry.summary: Hormonal birth control has allowed millions worldwide to manage their menstrual cycles and control their reproductive futures.

entry.title: Legionnaires' outbreak hits New York as officials rush to test water towers
entry.summary: To date, 46 people have been infected from breathing mist from bacteria-filled water coolers on the Upper East side.

entry.title: Rabid cat, bat, raccoons and skunks reported in these 4 Virginia counties
entry.summary: A rabid cat, bat, raccoons and skunks have been confirmed in parts of the Rappahannock-Rapidan Health District.

entry.title: Martha Lillard, last US polio patient using iron lung, dies at 78 in Oklahoma
entry.summary: Martha Lillard, the last U.S. polio patient using an iron lung, has died at 78 in Oklahoma. Her sister, Cindy McVey, attributes her death to long-haul COVID-19. Diagnosed with polio at 5, Lillard lived a remarkable life despite her challenges. She attended sc…

entry.title: Health officials confirm Nashville’s first measles case of 2026
entry.summary: The Metro Public Health Department issued an alert Friday about the first measles case of the year confirmed in Nashville, adding that the patient was in public during the "infectious period" over Fourth of July weekend.

entry.title: Box Elder County declares emergency amid 'severe' milk production loss
entry.summary: Box Elder County officials have declared a local state of emergency after a sample from a dairy farm in the county tested positive for highly pathogenic avian influenza.

entry.title: Taking Omega-3 for Your Brain Health? A New Study Says There Are Better Options
entry.summary: A new study finds high-dose omega-3 may boost DHA in the brain but not improve cognitive function or brain structure. Learn what this means for you.

entry.title: Michigan's cyclosporiasis outbreak is growing. Here's everything you need to know.
entry.summary: Doctors explain testing for the cyclospora parasite and detail how difficult it is to kill, what treats an infection and whether pets can get it.

entry.title: CSU San Marcos, NCTD Sprinter exposed to tuberculosis
entry.summary: The exposure may have occurred between Dec. 15, 2025, and May 14, 2026, at CSUSM's main campus.

entry.title: Modern Decor May Be Straining People’s Brains
entry.summary: Scientists say modern buildings, LED lighting, and repetitive patterns may overload the brain's visual processing system, causing headaches, nausea, and physical distress for millions.

entry.title: Ultrasound Breakthrough Helps Save Twins With Rare Syndrome
entry.summary: Noninvasive, high-intensity waves rebalance blood flow in womb for twin-to-twin transfusion syndrome

entry.title: Loudoun Co. health officials on alert after cat found with rabies
entry.summary: Health officials in Loudoun County, Virginia, are asking people who may have been exposed to a rabid cat in the South Riding area to contact their doctor as soon as possible.

entry.title: Jamestown Canyon virus found in mosquitoes in Salem, Fremont
entry.summary: Jamestown Canyon virus has been found in mosquitoes in Salem and Fremont, health officials said.

entry.title: Your resting heart rate could reveal more about your health than you think, doctors say
entry.summary: Here is what your resting heart rate reveals about cardiovascular health and when to see your doctor. Pulse checks and wearable devices can help with measuring the rate.

entry.title: Study shows how toxic RFK Jr.’s change to measles vaccine is for US toddlers
entry.summary: The children who get a combination shot are some of the most vulnerable.

entry.title: Urgent warning as brain-swelling disease surges in multiple Southern California neighborhoods
entry.summary: A dangerous mosquito-borne virus that can cause deadly brain swelling is surging across Orange County — and officials are now sending trucks into one hard-hit city to fight back.

entry.title: Bryan Johnson Says an Incurable Autoimmune Disease Has Changed His Longevity Mission
entry.summary: Bryan Johnson has built a public profile around trying to slow aging, measure his body in extreme detail, and push longevity science beyond normal wellness....
